evals.prompt: Fix this Dart error: The argument type 'String' can't be assigned to the parameter type 'int'. Error is in user_service.dart line 23.
evals.expected_output: Enters Fix mode, runs dart analyze, proposes 2-3 type conversion fixes, applies chosen fix, verifies zero errors
expectations: Detects Fix mode from the Dart analysis error
Proposes at least 2 fix approaches before applying
Does not use // ignore: to suppress the error
Runs dart analyze or checks diagnostics after the fix

evals.prompt: I'm getting 'Undefined name JsonDecoder' in my data_parser.dart file. Auto fix it.
evals.expected_output: Enters Fix mode, identifies missing import, adds dart:convert import, verifies
expectations: Detects Fix mode from the undefined name error
Identifies missing import as the root cause
Proposes adding the correct import (dart:convert)
Verifies with dart analyze after applying the fix

evals.prompt: My app crashes with 'Null check operator used on a null value' in profile_screen.dart. The stack trace points to line 45 where I access a user object.
evals.expected_output: Enters Quick mode, investigates null source, proposes 2-3 null safety fixes
expectations: Detects Quick mode from the runtime null assertion crash
Reads the affected file before proposing fixes
Proposes at least 2 solutions with risk levels
Waits for user to pick a solution before applying

evals.prompt: Getting 'setState() or markNeedsBuild() called during build' in my shopping_cart_page.dart. It happens when I add an item.
evals.expected_output: Enters Quick mode, identifies synchronous state mutation during build, proposes deferred or architectural fixes
expectations: Detects Quick mode from the widget build error
Identifies setState during build as the symptom pattern
Proposes deferring state change (addPostFrameCallback or Future.microtask) as one solution
Checks both the error site and the call site

evals.prompt: I have a Riverpod provider that causes my widget to rebuild every single frame. The UserListNotifier keeps triggering rebuilds even when the data hasn't changed. I've tried adding == overrides but it still happens. This is the second time I'm trying to fix this. Can you do a root cause analysis?
evals.expected_output: Enters Deep mode, investigates from 3+ angles, produces structured report with ranked causes, no code changes
expectations: Detects Deep mode from 'root cause analysis' and 2+ failed fix attempts
Investigates at least 3 angles (state management, widget lifecycle, rebuild triggers)
Produces a structured report with HIGH/MED/LOW ranked causes
Does not modify any project code (read-only investigation)
Cites file:line for findings

evals.prompt: We have a memory leak somewhere. The app's memory usage keeps growing over time. I suspect it's related to StreamControllers not being closed in our notification_service.dart, but I've already tried adding dispose calls twice and it didn't help.
evals.expected_output: Enters Deep mode, multi-angle investigation of stream lifecycle and subscriptions, structured report
expectations: Detects Deep mode from memory leak with 2+ failed fixes
Investigates stream subscription lifecycle as one angle
Checks for unclosed StreamControllers and dangling listeners
Produces investigation report saved to Documents/Debug/
Proposes solutions as WHAT/WHERE without code patches

evals.prompt: Add structured debug logging to my UserRepository.fetchUsers() method so I can trace the API call flow.
evals.expected_output: Enters Log mode, generates package:logger snippets with [UserRepository.fetchUsers] prefix, kDebugMode guard
expectations: Detects Log mode from 'add debug logging' request
Uses package:logger (log.d/i/w/e) not print() or debugPrint()
Includes structured [ClassName.methodName] prefix in log messages
Outputs as code block only, does not write to project files

evals.prompt: I need error boundary logging for my PaymentFormWidget. Add try-catch logging around the submission flow in the StatefulWidget.
evals.expected_output: Enters Log mode, generates error boundary logging with log.e, stack trace capture, kDebugMode guard
expectations: Detects Log mode from 'error boundary logging' request
Generates try-catch with log.e including error and stackTrace parameters
Guards verbose logging with kDebugMode or assert()
Does not log sensitive payment data (no card numbers, tokens)
